questions.assertion: Assertion (A): If f(x) and g(x) are two continuous functions, then their sum f(x) + g(x) is also continuous.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Since f(x) and g(x) are continuous, they satisfy the conditions of continuity.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the continuity of f(x) and g(x) directly implies the continuity of f(x) + g(x).

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): If f(x) is a differentiable function, then it is continuous.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Differentiability implies continuity because the derivative exists at every point.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because differentiability is a stronger condition than continuity, and differentiability implies continuity.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The function f(x) = |x| is continuous at x = 0.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The function f(x) = |x| is not differentiable at x = 0.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the function f(x) = |x| is continuous at x = 0, but it is not differentiable at x = 0.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The function f(x) = 1/x is continuous at x = 0.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): If f(x) and g(x) are two differentiable functions, then their product f(x)g(x) is also differentiable.

opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the differentiability of f(x) and g(x) directly implies the differentiability of f(x)g(x).

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The function f(x) = sin(x) is differentiable everywhere.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): If f(x) is a continuous function, then it is differentiable.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Differentiability implies continuity because the derivative exists at every point.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because continuity does not imply differentiability; there are many continuous functions that are not differentiable.
